prelude_surface_scope: SharedCell<T> identity linkage; exact shared-state rules are external
authority_fence.on_semantics: ActorOnDecl remains the existing grammar owner; this contract does not infer structural protocol conformance or add an alternate handler surface
authority_fence.await_semantics: await remains the existing explicit unary suspension owner over an independently admitted awaitable expression; this contract adds neither implicit await nor a task expression

rules.title: current concurrency surfaces retain their existing owners

task_surfaces: ordinary MessageSuffix whose selected identifier is spawn

rules.title: actor-owned state and boundary transfer remain explicit

contract.boundary_inputs: owned values with Transferable proof or explicitly shared values with Shareable proof
contract.isolation_axis: preserved through checking, API identity, MIR, and runtime evidence

rules.title: the ordered actor channel is internal and profile-bound

contract.channel_identity: ChannelId is derived from exactly the channel_key tuple and does not add a fourth semantic ordering component
contract.sequence_allocation: channel_sequence is allocated exactly once at successful enqueue commit and is strictly increasing within the channel_key

rules.title: current mailbox profiles select one exact immediate-rejection contract

contract.dynamic_capacity: reject in the minimum current profile
contract.logical_unbounded_meaning: no language-level capacity rejection; implementation storage remains finite and resource exhaustion is not converted into ActorMessageError.mailboxFull
contract.receiver_closed_before_reply: a successfully admitted request Task terminates through its declared ActorMessageError failure axis with ActorMessageError::receiverClosedBeforeReply

rules.title: actor message selection is not ordinary method dispatch

contract.selector_identity: resolved before enqueue against the admitted actor/protocol domain

rules.title: enqueue commit is the ownership transfer point

contract.successful_enqueue: receiver actor acquires each moved owner exactly once
contract.enqueue_commit_count: exactly one on an admitted attempt and zero on a rejected attempt
contract.cleanup_owner_count: exactly one on every terminal path

rules.title: one-way send and actor on declarations stay distinct from requests

contract.success_value: Result::ok with a Unit payload only after successful enqueue commit
contract.handler_binding: must be established by the existing checker and conformance authority; spelling alone is insufficient

rules.title: request and reply use one explicit correlated terminal channel

contract.message_expression_type: Result<Task<T>, error ActorMessageError>, where T is the selected request reply type
contract.admission_success: Result::ok(task) only after successful enqueue commit; source must extract Task<T> before await

field_contract.result_type: the selected request reply type T after normalization
field_contract.normalized_handler_error_set: the handler's exact normalized declared ErrorSet; Never is represented as the empty set
field_contract.cancellation_axis: a distinct non-Error control axis and never an ActorMessageError case
field_contract.isolation_owner: the selected actor/request isolation owner identity
field_contract.correlation_id: the non-forgeable per-admitted-request value identity created after enqueue commit; a module API digest records only the static per_value_non_forgeable policy marker

compatibility_and_join.default_rule: exact normalized static-field equality while preserving each value's non-forgeable correlation_id
compatibility_and_join.only_admitted_variance: an explicit admitted error-set-subsumption proof may widen normalized_handler_error_set; result_type, cancellation_axis, isolation_owner and terminal_transport_failure remain exact

contract.reply_type: must match the selected request requirement
contract.actor_stop_with_pending_request: must produce an explicit terminal outcome
contract.receiver_closed_before_reply: the admitted Task<T> terminates through its declared failure axis with ActorMessageError::receiverClosedBeforeReply

rules.title: standalone spawn and message spawn have distinct existing syntax owners

contract.standalone_body: the narrow TaskBody and TaskBodySequence grammar
contract.message_spawn_owner: ordinary MessageSuffix recognized by HIR when the selector is spawn

rules.title: a task scope owns every child until terminal join or cancellation

contract.scope_owner: StructuredTaskScope or an admitted TaskGroupStmt owner
contract.scope_exit_condition: every owned child is joined or cancelled and its required cleanup is complete
contract.task_handle_escape: reject unless a separately admitted owner transfer contract exists
contract.unobserved_child_failure: must remain visible to the scope terminal outcome
contract.task_group_collection_policy: requires an explicit admitted checker/runtime profile; no product default is claimed

rules.title: await is explicit and preserves responsibility across suspension

contract.live_borrow_rule: a borrow or inout value may cross only with an independently admitted proof
contract.actor_turn_suspension: the same ActorTurn retains its state-region and dequeue authority across await; no second mutating turn is admitted
contract.statically_proven_self_or_dependency_cyc: reject before MIR because the awaited progress requires the same active actor turn
contract.task_handle_repeat_await_policy: not created or changed by this contract

rules.title: cancellation is cooperative, distinct, and cleanup-bounded

contract.observation: only at an admitted cooperative cancellation boundary
contract.terminal_visibility: preserved in MIR and task/request outcome evidence

rules.title: existing scope modifiers do not manufacture hidden authority

contract.isolated: preserves the existing isolation boundary and grants no ambient capability
contract.shielded: does not convert, discard, or catch Cancellation and cannot bypass cleanup
contract.combination_admission: remains owned by the existing scope admission policy

contract.body_failure: retained as primary before cleanup failures
contract.child_failure_primary_order: ascending lexical spawn_index among terminal child failures observed by one scope
contract.child_failure_suppressed_order: ascending lexical spawn_index after the primary child failure
contract.cleanup_failure_order: append in actual deterministic LIFO cleanup execution order

rules.title: enqueue commit separates sender and actor ownership under cancellation

contract.cancellation_before_enqueue_commit: no message is enqueued and the sender retains moved owners
contract.cancellation_before_enqueue_result: Cancellation remains a distinct control outcome; it is not converted to ActorMessageError
contract.enqueue_commit_before_cancellation: the actor owns the enqueued payload and cancellation does not silently retract it
contract.one_way_after_commit: the Result::ok Unit outcome remains committed; later cancellation cannot rewrite it
contract.request_after_commit: Result::ok(Task<T>) remains committed; cancellation of the awaiting task is correlation-bound and does not retract the message

rules.title: concurrency outcomes require explicit MIR identities and events

required_event_families: cancellation request, observation, cleanup completion, and terminal cancellation
bounded admission success or immediate ActorMessageError rejection without channel_sequence
request creation and correlated terminal reply/failure/cancellation

rules.title: minimum happens-before edges are explicit and no global schedule is promised

contract.spawn_to_child_start: parent pre-spawn actions precede child start
contract.child_terminal_to_await_resume: child terminal actions and cleanup precede the awaiting continuation

rules.title: design-static coherence is not implementation or product evidence

unclosed_product_gates.topic: target-bound parser/checker/MIR/xVM execution receipt for bounded_reject_v1, logical_unbounded_v1, ActorMessageError and channel_sequence allocation
protocol requirement to ActorOnDecl/ActorRequestDecl conformance and handler identity algorithm
TaskGroupStmt join/collect policy and Task handle repeated-await rule
target-bound RCTS-V5 cancellation and complete MIR actor/task event lowering receipts
